run.name: Check licenses

head_commit.message:
fs/path: fix `EISDIR`/`EINVAL` errno precedence when opening directories

When opening an existing directory, Asterinas returned incorrect error
codes because type-related checks were missing in `path.open()`.

- `O_CREAT | O_DIRECTORY` on an existing directory returned `EACCES`
  or `EEXIST` instead of `EINVAL`. The check only existed in the
  `AtParent` branch (file not found), but was absent in the `Resolved`
  branch (file exists), so later checks triggered first.

- `O_CREAT` on an existing directory returned `EACCES` or `EEXIST`
  instead of `EISDIR`. No type check existed in `path.open()`, so
  permission or `O_EXCL` checks fired before the type check.

- Opening a directory with write mode (`O_WRONLY`/`O_RDWR`) returned
  `EACCES` instead of `EISDIR`. Without a pre-check, the `MAY_EXEC`
  permission check in `lookup_at_path()` triggered first when the
  directory lacked execute permission.

Fix by adding three early checks at the top of `path.open()`, before
any permission checks, matching Linux errno precedence:
1. `O_CREAT | O_DIRECTORY` → `EINVAL`
2. `O_CREAT` + target is directory → `EISDIR`
3. Write mode + target is directory → `EISDIR` (excluding `O_PATH`)

These fixes improve Linux syscall compatibility from 68.05% to 90.54%
(measured over ~2.8 million open/openat test cases).

run.display_title: fs/path: fix `EISDIR`/`EINVAL` errno precedence when opening directories

run.name: Test riscv64

head_commit.message:
test/fs: add regression test for symlink with trailing slash

Add FN_TEST cases in symlink.c to verify:
- open("symlink/", O_RDONLY | O_NOFOLLOW) succeeds when symlink
  points to a directory (trailing slash implies directory lookup,
  so the symlink must be followed even with O_NOFOLLOW)
- open("symlink", O_CREAT | O_EXCL | O_NOFOLLOW) returns EEXIST
  when the path is an existing symlink

Also fix the check order in Path::open: O_CREAT | O_EXCL must be
checked before O_NOFOLLOW to return EEXIST instead of ELOOP.

run.display_title: fs/path: fix symlink resolution with trailing slash
